name: LinkedIn: Profile
summary: Open a LinkedIn profile read-only (by vanity slug or full /in/ URL) and return name, headline, location, about, experience, and education.
description: Resolve a LinkedIn profile slug or full /in/ URL to a canonical profile URL, open it, and return a structured top-card plus About / Experience / Education summary. Read-only. Use for a quick read of who someone is and where they have worked. Degrades gracefully (missing fields become empty string or empty array).

profile.description: A LinkedIn vanity slug (e.g. jay-klause-3a2a75419) OR a full https://www.linkedin.com/in/<slug>/ URL.

steps.name: Open a same-origin LinkedIn tab so canonical navigation is cookie-safe
Resolve the slug or URL to a canonical /in/<slug>/ URL and navigate
Allow the profile page to hydrate
Allow the profile top card to render
Extract top card, About, Experience, and Education

help.summary: Open a LinkedIn profile read-only and return name, headline, location, about, experience, and education.

parameters.profile: A LinkedIn vanity slug (e.g. jay-klause-3a2a75419) OR a full /in/ URL.

examples.description: Open the profile by vanity slug and return its structured summary.
Same, passing a full /in/ URL (the slug is extracted automatically).

returns: { profile_url, name, headline, location, about, experience[], education[] } where experience and education are arrays of short strings (<=8).
notes: Read-only. Never connects, messages, follows, or posts.
Tolerates both a bare vanity slug and a full /in/ URL; the canonical URL is computed in-page before navigating.
Sections are located by their visible H2 heading text (About / Experience / Education) since LinkedIn class names are hashed per deploy.
Missing fields degrade to empty string or empty array.
